name: Grilled Asparagus
description: From Meathead: The Science of Great Barbecue and Grilling (Goldwyn), p.350.
recipeIngredient: 1 tbsp balsamic condimento or balsamic reduction
recipeInstructions: Snap off the woody bottoms (1-3 in).
Coat spears with oil, sprinkle with the rub and salt, shave the cheese into wide ribbons.
Grill two-zone ~325F on the indirect side; finish with the Parm ribbons and a balsamic drizzle.
author: Meathead Goldwyn

ai.cliffNotes: Two-zone grilling with indirect heat allows asparagus to cook through without charring, while the finishing touch of Parmigiano ribbons and balsamic adds richness and acidity.
keyTips: Snap woody bottoms before cooking—they won't soften and waste grill space
Use two-zone setup with indirect side as primary cooking zone to avoid burning the delicate spears
Add cheese and balsamic after grilling when spears are hot so Parm softens slightly and flavors meld
Shave cheese into wide ribbons rather than grating for better texture and visual appeal
